resolvedQuestions.label: KQ1 — 37-tool parity matrix
resolvedQuestions.evidence: iterations/iteration-001.md finding 1.1; 5 already ported, 32 port-able, 0 truly lost in handler sense

resolvedQuestions.label: KQ2 — Daemon-dependency audit
resolvedQuestions.evidence: iterations/iteration-002.md findings 2.1-2.3; 6 services S1..S6 catalogued, 24/37 daemon-free, 13/37 soft, 0 hard; per-arch loss table built

resolvedQuestions.label: KQ3 — MCP-only affordances and concrete CLI replacements
resolvedQuestions.evidence: iterations/iteration-003.md findings 3.1-3.7; 5 affordances A1..A5 catalogued; only A5 is lost in (a); 4x5 cross-runtime replacement matrix built

resolvedQuestions.label: KQ4 — Integration-surface migration
resolvedQuestions.evidence: iterations/iteration-004.md findings 4.1-4.7; 5 surfaces S1..S5; 28 files / ~125 refs; S1+S2+S4+S5 = 1-3 days mechanical; S3 (OpenCode tools: block) = 1-3 weeks gate

resolvedQuestions.label: KQ5 — Architecture comparison + go/no-go
resolvedQuestions.evidence: iterations/iteration-005.md findings 5.1-5.5; 5-dim scoring (a)=0.45, (b)=0.92, (c)=0.97; risk register 9 risks; **verdict: GO with architecture (c) — hybrid CLI that auto-spawns the daemon on demand**

keyFindings.label: 100% of the 37 MCP tools can be CLI-replaced in handler terms; 5 already ported today (generate-context, cli.ts stats/bulk-delete, reindex-embeddings, validate-memory-quality)
The handler layer is the parity surface, not the tool — the CLI just needs to reproduce parseArgs → validateToolArgs → handler → JSON.stringify(content)
memory_quick_search is just memory_search with defaults — no new handler
Causal and checkpoint tools (8 total) have no daemon requirement
6 daemon-resident services (S1..S6) catalogued; 24/37 tools are daemon-free, 13/37 are soft-dependent, 0 are hard-required
Architecture (a) loses 3 services (S1 warm embedder, S2 file-watcher, S4 RSS watchdog); (b) and (c) keep all 6
The single-writer lease (S5) is interprocess by design — all 3 architectures inherit it for free
Architecture (c) is the strict superset of (b) in feature retention and the strict superset of (a) in robustness
5 MCP-only affordances catalogued; only A5 is lost in (a); A1..A4 all replaced via per-runtime surfaces
OpenCode 'tools:' block is the only blocking external runtime dependency; Claude/Codex/Copilot use existing permission surfaces
Zod boundary validation is transport-agnostic; cost is one line per subcommand
Session-proxy replay (-32001/-32002) is preserved unchanged in (b)/(c); (a) implements its own ENOTCONN retry
5 migration surfaces catalogued; 28 files / ~125 references total
S1+S2+S4+S5 migration is mechanical: 1-3 days; S2 has 34 inline calls in manage.md requiring CLI flag-syntax rewrite
S3 (OpenCode 'tools:' block) is the migration critical path: 1-3 weeks if OpenCode ships, 2-3 days for CLI shim
5-dimension scoring: (a)=0.45, (b)=0.92, (c)=0.97. Architecture (c) is the unique strict-superset point in the 3-D Pareto space (feature retention × robustness × migration cost)
Risk register: (a) accumulates 1 HIGH + 4 MEDIUM risks; (b) and (c) accumulate 0 HIGH + 1 MEDIUM each

**VERDICT: GO with architecture (c) — hybrid CLI that auto-spawns the daemon on demand.** Migration = 13-16 days, 1 engineer; cost of (a) saving is 1 day but operational cost (R1/R3/R5/R6) is much higher

ruledOutDirections.direction: 11 CLIs framing as broad parity evidence
ruledOutDirections.reason: 10/11 are operational utilities, not user-facing tool substitutes

ruledOutDirections.direction: Treat KQ1 matrix as a sufficient zero-loss verdict
ruledOutDirections.reason: KQ3 protocol affordances must also be evaluated

ruledOutDirections.direction: A1 tool-schema auto-discovery as a uniform problem
A5 auto-surface as a single replacement problem

ruledOutDirections.reason: Inline tool calls in prompt bodies require CLI flag-syntax rewrite
OpenCode 'tools:' block is the migration critical path
Multi-process by design; CLI is one entry point, not one process
Composable per-tool and per-runtime; only S3 is truly atomic
Operational cost (R1, R3, R5, R6) exceeds migration saving
